title: Disciphania convolvulacea
taxonomy: Disciphania convolvulacea belongs to the family Menispermaceae. This plant is a species of flowering plant in the genus Disciphania. It is native to tropical regions of Central and South America. The classification of Disciphania convolvulacea is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, with the rank of species below it. The classification of this plant is also sometimes considered to be part of the order Ranunculales.
uses: Disciphania convolvulacea is traditionally used in medicine due to its reported properties as an anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial agent. It is also used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems. The plant is said to have anthelmintic properties, making it effective in eliminating parasites from the body. Some studies suggest that it may also possess antioxidant properties, which could help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. However, more research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal properties and potential applications.

intro: Disciphania convolvulacea, also known as the "Cayratia" or "Cayratia speciosa", is a perennial climber plant native to tropical Africa, belonging to the Cucurbitaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Disciphania convolvulacea include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Disciphania convolvulacea is commonly used in traditional medicine to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to alleviate symptoms of various health conditions.
